tables.description: Stores information about organizational departments within the company.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each department record.
Human-readable name of the department.

tables.description: Contains employee personal details and links to their department.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each employee record.
Full name of the employee as stored.
Reference to the department the employee belongs to.

tables.description: Records employee check-in and check-out times for each workday.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each attendance entry.
Reference to the employee whose attendance is recorded.
Timestamp when the employee clocked in for work.
Timestamp when the employee clocked out after work.
Date on which the attendance record applies.

tables.description: Defines job roles or positions available in the organization.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each role definition.
Descriptive name of the job role or position.

tables.description: Many-to‑many mapping of employees to the roles they hold.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each employee-role assignment.
Reference to the employee holding the role.
Reference to the role assigned to the employee.

tables.description: Defines work shifts with their start and end times.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each work shift pattern.
Label identifying the shift, e.g., Morning, Night.
Time of day when the shift begins.
Time of day when the shift ends.

tables.description: Assigns employees to specific shifts on particular dates.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each employee shift schedule.
Reference to the employee assigned to the schedule.
Reference to the shift assigned on the schedule date.
Date on which the scheduled shift occurs.

tables.description: Tracks employee leave requests, including type, dates, and approval status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each leave request.
Reference to the employee requesting leave.
Category of leave being requested (e.g., sick, vacation).
First date of the requested leave period.
Last date of the requested leave period.
Current approval status of the leave request.

tables.description: Logs training completed by employees, with completion dates.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each training record entry.
Reference to the employee who completed the training.
Name or title of the completed training program.
Date when the training was finished by the employee.

tables.description: Catalogs categories of equipment (e.g., tug, loader) used in operations.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each equipment type.
Descriptive name of the equipment category.

tables.description: Lists individual equipment items, their current status, and type.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each equipment record.
Human‑readable name or label of the equipment.
Current operational state of the equipment (e.g., active, maintenance).
Reference to the type/category the equipment belongs to.

tables.description: Records assignments of equipment to employees, with assignment and return dates.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each equipment‑assignment record.
FK to equipment indicating which item is assigned.
FK to employee who receives the equipment.
Date when the equipment was issued to the employee.
Date when the equipment was returned (nullable if still out).

tables.description: Captures requests for maintenance on equipment, including description and status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each maintenance request.
FK to equipment that needs maintenance.
Free‑form text detailing the requested maintenance work.
Current state of the request (e.g., open, in progress, closed).

tables.description: Stores historical records of performed maintenance actions on equipment.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each maintenance log entry.
FK to equipment that was serviced.
Date when the maintenance activity was performed.
Additional notes or comments about the maintenance performed.

tables.description: Contains flight identifiers and their current operational status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each flight record.
Airline‑assigned code identifying the flight (e.g., AA123).
Current status of the flight (e.g., scheduled, departed, cancelled).

tables.description: Defines airport gates, their numbers, and availability status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each gate record.
Alphanumeric label of the gate (e.g., A12).
Operational status of the gate (e.g., open, closed, under maintenance).

tables.description: Defines aircraft parking stands, their numbers, and availability status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each stand record.
Alphanumeric identifier of the aircraft stand (e.g., S5).
Current status of the stand (e.g., available, occupied).

tables.description: Links flights to gates at specific times for boarding/deboarding.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each gate‑assignment record.
FK to gate indicating which gate is used.
FK to flight assigned to the gate.
Timestamp when the gate assignment was made.

tables.description: Links flights to stands at specific times for parking.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each stand‑assignment record.
FK to stand indicating which stand is used.
FK to flight assigned to the stand.
Timestamp when the stand assignment was made.

tables.description: Represents the turnaround process for a flight, tracking start and target departure times.

columns.description: Unique identifier for each turnaround operation record.
FK to flight whose turnaround is being tracked.
Timestamp when turnaround operations begin.
Planned timestamp for flight departure after turnaround.
Current status of the turnaround (e.g., in progress, completed).

tables.description: Lists checklist items tied to a turnaround operation and their completion status.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for the checklist entry.
Foreign key linking the checklist to its turnaround operation.
Brief description of the specific task to be performed.
Indicates whether the checklist item has been finished.

tables.description: Tracks boarding‑related tasks for a turnaround, such as gate readiness and baggage loading.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for the boarding task record.
Foreign key referencing the associated turnaround operation.
Boolean flag showing if the gate is prepared for boarding.
Boolean flag indicating whether baggage has been loaded onto aircraft.
Current state of the boarding task (e.g., pending, completed).

tables.description: Records fueling details for a turnaround, including required and added fuel amounts.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for the refueling task record.
Foreign key linking to the turnaround operation being refueled.
Amount of fuel left in the aircraft tanks before refueling.
Amount of fuel needed to reach the desired fuel level.
Quantity of fuel actually added during the refueling process.
Current state of the refueling task (e.g., in progress, done).

tables.description: Stores catering service details for a turnaround, including vendor and timing.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for the catering task record.
Foreign key linking the catering task to its turnaround.
Name of the vendor providing catering services for the flight.
Timestamp when the catering service begins.
Timestamp when the catering service ends.
Current state of the catering task (e.g., scheduled, completed).

tables.description: Records cleaning tasks performed during a turnaround, assigned to an employee.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for the cleaning task record.
Foreign key linking the cleaning task to its turnaround.
Identifier of the employee assigned to perform the cleaning.
Timestamp when the cleaning activity starts.
Timestamp when the cleaning activity ends.
Current state of the cleaning task (e.g., pending, finished).

tables.description: Holds baggage records linked to flights, with tag numbers and current status.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for each baggage item.
Foreign key linking baggage to the flight it belongs on.
Unique code printed on the baggage tag for identification.
Current status of the baggage (e.g., loaded, unloaded).

tables.description: Logs the time each bag is loaded onto a flight.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for the baggage loading event.
Foreign key referencing the baggage being loaded.
Foreign key linking the loading event to the flight.
Timestamp when the baggage was loaded onto the aircraft.

tables.description: Logs the time each bag is unloaded from a flight.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for the baggage unloading event.
Foreign key referencing the baggage being unloaded.
Foreign key linking the unloading event to the flight.
Timestamp when the baggage was unloaded from the aircraft.

tables.description: Records scanning events of baggage at various locations and timestamps.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for each baggage scan record.
Foreign key referencing the baggage that was scanned.
Location where the baggage scan took place (e.g., checkpoint).
Timestamp when the baggage scan occurred.

tables.description: Tracks baggage that has been reported lost, including status and description.

columns.description: Primary key identifier for the lost baggage report.
Foreign key linking the report to the specific baggage item.
Current status of the lost baggage case (e.g., open, closed).
Free‑form text describing the circumstances of the loss.

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: An employee belongs to one department (ManyToOne), can have multiple roles (ManyToMany via employee_role), has many attendance records (OneToMany), can be assigned many schedules (OneToMany), may submit many leave requests (OneToMany), can participate in many training records (OneToMany), and may be assigned to various turnaround tasks (ManyToMany via task tables).

relationships.name: record belongs to
relationships.description: Each attendance record belongs to a single employee (ManyToOne).

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each employee_role belongs to a employee

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each employee_role belongs to a role

relationships.name: entry assigns one shift to
relationships.description: Each schedule entry assigns one shift to one employee (ManyToMany via schedule).

relationships.name: entry assigns one shift to
relationships.description: Each schedule entry assigns one shift to one employee (ManyToMany via schedule).

relationships.name: made by
relationships.description: A leave request is made by a single employee (ManyToOne).

relationships.name: associated with
relationships.description: A training record is associated with a single employee (ManyToOne).

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: Each equipment belongs to one equipment type (ManyToOne) and can have many maintenance requests (OneToMany).

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each equipment_assignment belongs to a equipment

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each equipment_assignment belongs to a employee

relationships.name: for a specific piece of
relationships.description: A maintenance request is for a specific piece of equipment (ManyToOne).

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each maintenance_history belongs to a equipment

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each gate_assignment belongs to a gate

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each gate_assignment belongs to a flight

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each stand_assignment belongs to a stand

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each stand_assignment belongs to a flight

relationships.name: linked to
relationships.description: A turnaround operation is linked to exactly one flight (OneToOne) and may involve many employees for various tasks (ManyToMany via task tables).

relationships.name: references
relationships.description: each turnaround_checklist references turnaround_operation via turnaround_id

relationships.name: references
relationships.description: each boarding_task references turnaround_operation via turnaround_id

relationships.name: references
relationships.description: each refueling_task references turnaround_operation via turnaround_id

relationships.name: references
relationships.description: each catering_task references turnaround_operation via turnaround_id

relationships.name: references
relationships.description: each cleaning_task references turnaround_operation via turnaround_id

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each cleaning_task belongs to a employee

relationships.name: associated with
relationships.description: Each baggage item is associated with a single flight (ManyToOne).

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each baggage_loading belongs to a baggage

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each baggage_loading belongs to a flight

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each baggage_unloading belongs to a baggage

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each baggage_unloading belongs to a flight

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each baggage_scan belongs to a baggage

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: each lost_baggage belongs to a baggage

entity_relationships.related_entities: Department, Role, Attendance, Schedule, LeaveRequest, TrainingRecord, TurnaroundOperation
entity_relationships.business_meaning: An employee belongs to one department (ManyToOne), can have multiple roles (ManyToMany via employee_role), has many attendance records (OneToMany), can be assigned many schedules (OneToMany), may submit many leave requests (OneToMany), can participate in many training records (OneToMany), and may be assigned to various turnaround tasks (ManyToMany via task tables).
Each attendance record belongs to a single employee (ManyToOne).
A role can be assigned to many employees (ManyToMany via employee_role).
A shift can be assigned to many schedule entries (OneToMany).
Each schedule entry assigns one shift to one employee (ManyToMany via schedule).
A leave request is made by a single employee (ManyToOne).
A training record is associated with a single employee (ManyToOne).
An equipment type can have many equipment items (OneToMany).
Each equipment belongs to one equipment type (ManyToOne) and can have many maintenance requests (OneToMany).
A maintenance request is for a specific piece of equipment (ManyToOne).
A flight can use many gates and stands over time (ManyToMany via gate_assignment and stand_assignment), has exactly one turnaround operation (OneToOne), and may have many baggage items (OneToMany).
A gate can be assigned to many flights (ManyToMany via gate_assignment).
A stand can be assigned to many flights (ManyToMany via stand_assignment).
A turnaround operation is linked to exactly one flight (OneToOne) and may involve many employees for various tasks (ManyToMany via task tables).
Each baggage item is associated with a single flight (ManyToOne).

business_processes.tables: department, employee, attendance, role, employee_role, shift, schedule, leave_request, training_record
flight, gate, stand, gate_assignment, stand_assignment, turnaround_operation, turnaround_checklist, boarding_task, refueling_task, catering_task, cleaning_task

use_cases.name: Shift Assignment
use_cases.description: Assign a specific shift to an employee for a given date, creating a schedule entry.

use_cases.name: Attendance Tracking
use_cases.description: Record whether an employee was present, absent, or late on a particular day.

use_cases.name: Leave Request Management
use_cases.description: Submit and track employee leave requests, including approval status and dates.

use_cases.name: Equipment Maintenance
use_cases.description: Create maintenance requests for equipment and track their history.

use_cases.name: Flight Turnaround and Baggage Handling
use_cases.description: Manage the turnaround operations for a flight, assign gates and stands, and track baggage loading, unloading, scanning, and lost baggage incidents.
use_cases.involved_tables: flight, gate_assignment, stand_assignment, turnaround_operation, boarding_task, refueling_task, catering_task, cleaning_task, baggage, baggage_loading, baggage_unloading, baggage_scan, lost_baggage

sample_queries.question: List all employees working in the 'Operations' department.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name FROM employee e JOIN department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id WHERE d.name = 'Operations';

SELECT g.gate_number, COUNT(*) AS flight_count FROM gate_assignment ga JOIN gate g ON ga.gate_id = g.gate_id JOIN flight f ON ga.flight_id = f.flight_id WHERE DATE(f.departure_time) = CURRENT_DATE GROUP BY g.gate_number ORDER BY flight_count DESC;

SELECT et.name AS equipment_type, COUNT(mr.request_id) AS request_count FROM maintenance_request mr JOIN equipment e ON mr.equipment_id = e.equipment_id JOIN equipment_type et ON e.equipment_type_id = et.equipment_type_id WHERE mr.request_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY et.name ORDER BY request_count DESC;
SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name FROM schedule s JOIN employee e ON s.employee_id = e.employee_id JOIN shift sh ON s.shift_id = sh.shift_id WHERE sh.name = 'Night Shift' AND s.date = '2025-08-15';

SELECT f.flight_number, COUNT(bs.scan_id) AS total_scans FROM baggage_scan bs JOIN baggage b ON bs.baggage_id = b.baggage_id JOIN flight f ON b.flight_id = f.flight_id GROUP BY f.flight_number ORDER BY total_scans DESC;
